learning_outcomes.description: Critically evaluate existing literature in a chosen field interpreting research findings accurately, drawing appropriate conclusions, identifying gaps and limitations to provide a nuanced understanding of current knowledge and appropriate research methodologies.
Critically evaluate the strengths and weaknesses of different methodological approaches used in studies and assess the implications of these to plan future research.

Design, develop, and articulate a well-structured research proposal which incorporates an answerable research question, a research plan, the identification of appropriate sampling techniques, and an outline of data collection and analysis.

Demonstrate advanced communication skills to articulate complex ideas clearly, structure arguments logically, and use appropriate academic conventions and referencing styles to effectively present and disseminate research findings.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment 1 Part A. Research Question + Annotated Bibliography
assessments.task_description:
For this task, you will develop a research question that has relevance to your program’s discipline and prepare an annotated bibliography of three (3) empirical studies that are relevant to your research question.

Part A. Research Question + Reflection (300 words)

You will define a research question that you have developed as a result of engaging with the literature and identifying a gap that is relevant for your discipline.

The research gap could relate to topics that have not previously been studied or topics that have been studied but for which notable gaps remain, such as:

·       under-researched populations,

·       methodological limitations,

·       inconsistent findings,

·       lack of application to specific practice contexts.

To develop your research question, you will:

1.     Engage with peer-reviewed literature that is relevant to your discipline/profession to identify a research topic that has the potential to inform practice.

2.     Develop a draft research question.

3.     Identify 3 key articles that cumulatively provide a rationale for your research topic.

4.     Input your key articles and your draft research question into Co-pilot and ask for feedback on your research question, together with three alternative research questions, based on the key articles.

5.     Evaluate the options provided by Co-pilot and finalise your research question.

6.   Complete the “Part A. Research question development and refinement summary” document and include this with your final submission.

Part B. Annotated Bibliography (900 words)

Develop an annotated bibliography for the three key articles you used to develop your research question.

For each study you include in your annotated bibliography, you will provide:

·       a summary of the study’s research question/s, research design, research methodology, and findings; and

·       an evaluation of the study by noting methodological strengths and weaknesses.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please submit your Research Question Reflection Sheet and Annotated Bibliography as a single document with an Assessment Coversheet, via the Turnitin submission link in Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This task builds on Part A of this assessment. The aim of this task is to demonstrate your ability to design, develop, and articulate a well-structured research proposal which incorporates:

·       an answerable research question (the one you developed in Part A);

·       a critical summary of relevant literature (the literature you identified in Part A);

·       a description of your proposed research method;

·       an overview of the relevant ethical considerations;

·       a discussion of the limitations of the study;

·       a consideration of the potential impact of the study;

·       a dissemination plan; and

·       an optional reflection on the process of developing a research proposal.

For this task, you will develop a research proposal of 1800 words. Your proposal should include the following components/sections.

Title: Your title should provide a comprehensive yet succinct overview of your research study. It should relate to your research question but is not your research question.

Literature review (600 words): Your literature review should incorporate the studies you have evaluated in Assessment 1 Part A, as well as other relevant research which has informed the development of your study.

The literature review should develop a rationale for your study by identifying a clear gap in the current understanding of your topic, and a strong justification for the importance of your specific research question.

Your literature review will conclude with a statement of your research question, which must have relevance for your program’s discipline (i.e., counselling or social work). If you have hypotheses based on your research question, you will also state your hypotheses in the concluding paragraph of your literature review.

Research design and methodology (600 words): You will describe the research design and methodology you intend to employ in your study. For each aspect of your design, provide justification for your choices, and ensure there is alignment between your research question and all aspects of your design.

This will include descriptions of:

·      research paradigm (qualitative, quantitative, mixed methods);

·      research methods (longitudinal; cohort; experiment; ethnographic etc);

·      participants – including how many participants; demographic characteristics; inclusion and exclusion criteria (if relevant);

·      recruitment strategy;

·      data collection methods and plan; and

·      data analysis approach/es.

Ethical considerations (200 words): In addition to identifying the ethical considerations that are inherent in all research studies with human participants, you will identify and describe any specific ethical considerations that are pertinent to your study. In addition, you will identify your plan to mitigate any potential risks associated with those ethical considerations. This mitigation plan should be based on careful consideration of the specific risks associated with your study, and, where possible, supported by evidence.

Limitations (150 words): You will identify the limitations of your study and explain why/how these limitations may impact on the study. Further, you will provide a rationale which justifies your study, despite the noted limitations.

Potential impact (200 words): You will explain, with reference to your research question and the design of your study, the likely contribution of your study. Ensure you are realistic when discussing this impact, as it is important that you do not overstate the potential contribution of a single study. Rather than “proving” anything, your study may “contribute to knowledge” or “provide further evidence in support” or “clarify key contributors”. All of these outcomes are valuable impacts of research.

Dissemination plan (50 words): You will identify the way/s in which you will communicate your study’s findings to relevant audiences and stakeholders. It is appropriate to say that you will publish your findings in a peer-reviewed journal. It is also recommended that you consider other ways of disseminating your findings. The methods you choose for this dissemination may depend on the stakeholders of your study i.e., who is likely to benefit from knowing about your study and how will you ensure they are able to access those findings?

Reflection (optional): Please identify three (3) aspects of your assignment you would like specific feedback on.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please submit your Annotated Bibliography via the Turnitin submission link in Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment 2. Oral Defence of Research Proposal
assessments.task_description:
The focus of this assessment will be the Research Proposal you have prepared for this course. After submitting your Research Proposal, you will undertake a brief online interview (15 minutes) with an examiner. Your examiner will ask you 4-6 questions about your Research Proposal. These questions will explore a range of topics, including:

·      The rationale for your research question.

·      Your research design, including the research paradigm you have chosen and your justification for that choice.

·      Your data collection and data analysis approaches (including their alignment with your research question).

·      Ethical considerations of your study, including how you intend to mitigate any noted concerns.

·      Limitations of your study.

·      Potential impact of your study, including its relevance for your discipline.

·      Dissemination plans for your study, including who may benefit from learning about your study and how you intend to ensure they are able to access the findings.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
